mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.79% is near historical lows, signaling an accommodative monetary stance. Sustained low rates compress long-term discount rates and reduce term premia, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline over the next 20 trading days as investors extend duration exposure.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 470,844 million dollars and housing starts (HOUST) at 1,288 thousand units indicate robust consumer demand. Strong demand growth signals inflation expectations and economic overheating, causing the Fed to maintain or raise rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST > 1,250 thousand units
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 20 basis points despite both RSAFS and HOUST meeting trigger thresholds, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.5% combined with payroll employment at 145,858 thousand persons indicates a tight labor market. Tight labor conditions drive wage growth and inflation expectations, prompting the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, which elevates 10-year Treasury yields over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.7% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change > 100 thousand
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage pressure
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 15 basis points despite both labor indicators meeting trigger conditions, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 103.7332 (index 2017=100) shows supply-side capacity utilization above trend. Elevated production combined with low unemployment signals potential supply constraints and inflation, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as markets price in persistent inflation and higher terminal rates over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 20 basis points despite INDPRO remaining above 103.5 with positive momentum, or signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 243.752 (index 1982-1984=100) combined with accommodative federal funds rate at 0.79% creates negative real rates. Negative real rates signal monetary policy is too loose relative to inflation, causing markets to expect future rate hikes, which pushes 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 25 basis points despite negative real rates persisting, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 16,813.3 billion chained 2017 dollars represents solid economic growth. Sustained GDP growth above trend signals strong demand and inflation risks, causing the Fed to maintain restrictive policy and 10-year Treasury yields to remain elevated or rise over the next 60 trading days as markets price in persistent rate pressure.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 50 basis points over 60 trading days despite GDP growth remaining above 2.0% annualized, or signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.5% is below the estimated natural rate of unemployment (approximately 4.7%), indicating labor market slack has been absorbed. Sub-natural unemployment combined with elevated payroll growth signals wage-driven inflation, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as markets expect the Fed to maintain or tighten policy over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with sub-natural unemployment
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 20 basis points despite UNRATE remaining below 4.6%, or signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1,288 thousand units combined with retail sales (RSAFS) at 470,844 million dollars show broad-based demand strength. When both demand indicators accelerate simultaneously, it signals economic momentum that forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise over the next 5 trading days as markets front-run policy expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change > 2.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month change > 0.8%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 15 basis points over 5 trading days despite both HOUST and RSAFS meeting acceleration thresholds, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
